experiment: H_LOGIC_EXTRACTION — Operational Logic as Topology in Formal Proof Systems
description: Test whether IRDME extracts operational logic from formal proof structure without symbolic translation. Dataset: Lean 4 mathlib4 multilayer graph. d1=import_graph (declared logical dependency), d3=proof_co_development (behavioral proof construction coupling).

hypotheses.statement: Operational logic is topologically recoverable: declared logical dependency (import_graph) and behavioral proof coupling (proof_co_development) have strong positive hub persistence (r >= 0.70, p < 0.05).
The top hub in declared dependency (import_graph) is Algebra (rank #1), representing axiomatic convergence in formal mathematics.
The top hub in behavioral proof coupling (proof_co_development) is Analysis (rank #1), indicating operational proof traffic concentrates on analytical infrastructure.

Logic-role divergence exists: at least 3 modules have cross-layer hub-rank divergence >= 8 between import_graph and proof_co_development, indicating distinct logical roles between declared and operational proof structures.

notes: H_LOGIC_EXTRACTION v1 extends F9 from law-replication to logic-extraction: topology is treated as operational logic geometry. Pre-registration required before run. Prediction is intentionally strict (r>=0.70) to make failure informative.
